evals.prompt: We have a team of ML specialists building a recommendation engine that other product teams depend on. What team type should this be, and how should those product teams interact with it?
evals.expected_output: Should identify this as a Complicated Subsystem team due to specialist expertise requirements, recommend X-as-a-Service interaction mode for product teams consuming the engine, and explain why a stream-aligned team couldn't own this.

assertions.name: identifies-complicated-subsystem-team
assertions.description: Response correctly identifies the ML team as a Complicated Subsystem team

assertions.name: recommends-xaas-interaction
assertions.description: Response recommends X-as-a-Service as the interaction mode for product teams consuming the engine

assertions.name: explains-reasoning
assertions.description: Response explains WHY this team type fits (specialist expertise, complexity beyond stream-aligned team capacity)

evals.prompt: Our platform team keeps getting pulled into every stream-aligned team's work because the teams can't use the platform self-sufficiently. What Team Topologies patterns apply and what should we change?
evals.expected_output: Should identify this as a failure of X-as-a-Service interaction mode and TVP principles — the platform isn't self-service enough. Should recommend improving platform usability/documentation, potentially using enabling teams to onboard stream-aligned teams, and applying TVP to simplify the platform.

assertions.name: diagnoses-xaas-failure
assertions.description: Response recognizes this as a broken X-as-a-Service interaction mode

assertions.name: mentions-tvp
assertions.description: Response mentions Thinnest Viable Platform or platform simplification

assertions.name: suggests-enabling-team
assertions.description: Response suggests using an enabling team (or facilitation mode) to help stream-aligned teams become self-sufficient

evals.prompt: We want to help our frontend teams adopt better testing practices. Should we create a separate QA team to own this, or is there a different approach?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend against a permanent QA team (creates handoff dependency), and suggest using an enabling team with facilitation interaction mode to temporarily coach frontend teams until they are self-sufficient. May reference cognitive load and the principle of eliminating team dependencies.

assertions.name: recommends-enabling-team
assertions.description: Response recommends an enabling team rather than a permanent QA team

assertions.name: uses-facilitation-mode
assertions.description: Response mentions facilitation as the appropriate interaction mode

assertions.name: explains-dependency-risk
assertions.description: Response explains the risk of creating a permanent dependency on a separate QA team
